heading: Duties of consular officers related to insubordination
label: 11503 Duties of consular officers related to insubordination
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)1150346:703Section 11503 delegates to consular officers certain responsibilities related to insubordination of seamen on United States vessels.

children.label: a
children.text: A consular officer shall use every means to discountenance insubordination on vessels of the United States, including employing the aid of local authorities.

children.label: b
children.text: When a seaman is accused of insubordination, a consular officer shall inquire into the facts and proceed as provided in . If the consular officer discharges the seaman, the officer shall endorse the agreement required by this part and enter in the vessel’s official logbook the cause and particulars of the discharge.section 11106 of this title
